\chapter{Methods}

\section{Negative Binomial Mixture Model}

We model the observed UMI count $Y_{ij}$ for cell $i$ and gene $j$ as a
mixture of a zero-inflated component and a negative binomial component:

\begin{equation}
Y_{ij} \sim \pi_{ij} \delta_0 + (1 - \pi_{ij}) \text{NB}(\mu_{ij}, \phi_j)
\end{equation}

where $\pi_{ij}$ is the dropout probability, $\mu_{ij}$ is the mean
expression, and $\phi_j$ is the gene-specific dispersion.
